text.title: Overview and core principle
text.summary: The orchestrator builds a complete AI media publishing pipeline from scratch (Telegram channel + static site + automation) by walking the operator through a structured discovery interview, then scaffolding a project that mirrors the centralized pattern proven across four production outlets: neromedia, pashtelka, longlife, ender.

rule.statement: Every new media outlet MUST conform to the centralized pattern: pipeline code in the outlet repo, scheduling and monitoring delegated to the central media-manager service. The orchestrator never adds cron entries directly to the host machine.
rule.rationale: Outlets drift when each one owns its own scheduler. A central control plane (media-manager) gives a single place to start, stop, monitor, and command outlets through a single Telegram bot. Per-outlet cron leads to silent failures, divergent timing, and double-publish bugs.

rule.statement: The orchestrator MUST collect ALL discovery interview answers before proposing a format or scaffolding any code.
rule.rationale: Decisions like single-language vs multi-language, automation level, and source mix change the pipeline complexity by an order of magnitude. Proposing early forces re-work; gathering all answers first makes the proposal a one-shot.

section.title: Role split
section.p: Three actors share the work. The orchestrator runs the interview, proposes the plan, and scaffolds the project. The user picks identity, voice, sources, and automation level. The central media-manager owns scheduling (cron), monitoring, and operator commands once the outlet is registered.

list.item: Orchestrator: discovery interview, format proposal, project scaffolding, infrastructure setup, registration in media-manager.
User: media identity, topics, audience, brand voice, source preferences, automation level, technical constraints (domain, hosting, budget).
Media-manager: cron schedules per outlet, health monitoring, morning briefing, agent commands (/status, /logs, /generate).

section.title: Reference outlets
section.p: Four production implementations the new outlet should mirror:

list.item: neromedia-faion-net — AI/tech news, 8 languages, 8 Telegram channels.
pashtelka-faion-net — Ukrainian diaspora in Portugal, single channel.
longlife-faion-net — health and longevity, Ukrainian, comic mascot Vita.
ender-faion-net — Roblox media for kids, Ukrainian plus English, characters EnderFaion and FaionEnder.

section.p: Central control plane lives at the media-manager-faion-net project. Templates used by this workflow live in the sibling templates/ folder (relative to this content directory).

rule.statement: User-facing strings during the interview and any operator chat live in Ukrainian. Code, prompt files, schemas, commit messages, and CHANGELOG entries live in English.
rule.rationale: Ukrainian for the human keeps the interview natural; English for everything machine-touchable saves roughly 30% of tokens and keeps grep mechanical.

text.reference: Pipeline, prompt, schema, gatsby, admin, and script templates rendered during scaffolding.
